title: Callipteris ridleyi
taxonomy: Callipteris ridleyi is classified within the plant division Pteridophyta, commonly known as ferns. It belongs to the family Matoniaceae, a group of ancient ferns that are mostly extinct today. The genus Callipteris is further divided into several species, with C. ridleyi being one of them. This classification is based on the plant's morphology and evolutionary relationships with other ferns. C. ridleyi is considered a rare and endangered species due to its limited geographical range and habitat.

intro: Callipteris ridleyi, also known as Kadam tree, is a deciduous tree native to India and Southeast Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can help to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and protect against oxidative stress, thereby benefiting overall health. The bioactive constituents of Callipteris ridleyi include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties.
